mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate declines below 1.0% signal monetary accommodation that reduces equity discount rates and increases present value of future corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:current FEDFUNDS=0.09% indicates deep accommodation regime
regime_hint: monetary easing cycle with rates near zero lower bound
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS < 1.0%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate above 7.0% indicates slack labor market conditions that constrain wage growth and corporate cost pressures, allowing equity valuations to expand and producing positive forward returns over 60 trading days as labor slack persists.
evidence_refs: fact:current UNRATE=7.4% signals elevated labor slack
regime_hint: post-shock recovery with persistent unemployment above natural rate
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are not significantly positive (mean return < 0.5%) when UNRATE > 7.0%, or correlation between UNRATE and forward returns reverses sign over any 18-month period.

mechanism_narrative: Real retail sales growth decelerates below 2% annualized rate, signaling weakening consumer demand that triggers equity repricing downward as forward earnings expectations compress over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:current RSAFS=424481.0M suggests retail demand level
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances despite weak retail sales signal, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production index declines month-over-month while unemployment rises simultaneously, indicating stagflationary pressure that compresses equity risk appetite and produces negative 20-day forward returns as investors reduce cyclical exposure.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines > 0.5% MoM AND UNRATE rises > 0.2pp in same month
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of co-triggered instances, or the joint signal shows Rank IC < 0.01 when tested independently against single-series alternatives.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts decline below 800k annualized units while real GDP growth remains below 2% annualized, indicating synchronized demand weakness across residential and aggregate output that produces negative 60-day forward returns as recession risk rises.
candidate_trigger: HOUST < 800k AND GDPC1 growth rate < 2% annualized
regime_hint: synchronized demand contraction or pre-recession environment
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of co-triggered instances, or the interaction signal shows no incremental predictive power beyond GDPC1 alone (Rank IC difference < 0.01).

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment growth accelerates above 200k monthly while unemployment remains stable below 7.5%, signaling robust labor market momentum that supports consumer spending and produces positive 5-day forward returns as growth expectations rise.
regime_hint: expansion phase with tight labor market conditions
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in more than 45% of co-triggered instances, or the interaction effect is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression against direction_5d.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation measured by CPI rises above 3.5% annualized while federal funds rate remains below 2.0%, indicating real rates are negative and monetary policy is accommodative, producing positive 60-day forward returns as investors seek equity hedges against currency debasement.
evidence_refs: fact:current FEDFUNDS=0.09% indicates deep negative real rates
regime_hint: inflationary environment with accommodative monetary policy
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in more than 50% of co-triggered instances, or real rate signal shows no incremental explanatory power beyond nominal FEDFUNDS (Rank IC difference < 0.015).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production index rises above 100 (2017=100 baseline) while inflation remains below 2.5%, indicating productivity-driven growth without cost-push pressure that supports equity valuations and produces positive 20-day forward returns as margin expansion expectations rise.
regime_hint: non-inflationary expansion with supply-side productivity gains
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of co-triggered instances, or the interaction signal shows Rank IC < 0.02 when tested against both single-series alternatives over any 2-year rolling window.
